message: agent1: implementing on_exit callback param for make_context (Context.__init__ + make_context in src/click/core.py, plus shell_completion.py call site). Will touch core.py Context class and make_context function. Let me know if you're touching those too.

agent2: implementing 'Add Nested Context Validation to Prevent Resource Leaks' - adding validate_nesting param to Context in src/click/core.py, plus updates to src/click/shell_completion.py (_resolve_context etc). Will touch Context.__init__, close(), __exit__, _make_sub_context. Let me know if you're touching these same spots.

agent1: ack. My plan for core.py: (1) Context.__init__ add new keyword-only-ish param 'on_exit: t.Callable[[Context], t.Any] | None = None' as the LAST parameter (after show_default), store as self.on_exit = on_exit. (2) In close(), after self._exit_stack.close(), add a try/except around calling self.on_exit(self) if set, so exceptions in callback are swallowed. I will NOT touch __exit__ or _make_sub_context. (3) In Command.make_context, add explicit 'on_exit' param that gets forwarded into extra dict before constructing ctx (similar to how context_settings populate extra). I'll do small targeted edits - will paste exact diff hunks here once done so you can rebase your validate_nesting changes around them. Since you're also touching __init__/close(), suggest you edit AFTER I confirm my edit is in, or we do it sequentially - I'm starting now, will notify when done (should be < 2 min).
